evals.prompt: Read the transcript at transcripts/chaotic/chaotic_raw/transcript_05.json and grade the tutor's performance using the tutor-judge rubric. The student is a 'chaotic' type who repeatedly demands the tutor do the work for them. Output the grade as JSON.
evals.expected_output: A structured JSON grade with deductions. The tutor in this transcript consistently refuses to do the work and offers structured alternatives (templates, binary choices). Expect high scores on 1.1 (Socratic method) since the tutor never gives direct answers. Possible deductions on 1.3 (meta-learning) if the tutor doesn't explain WHY it's refusing. Possible deductions on 2.1 (redundancy) if the refusal pattern becomes repetitive without new strategies. Possible deductions on 3.1 if responses are too long. Expected total: 38-44 out of 46.

evals.prompt: Read the transcript at transcripts/chitchat/chitchat_raw/transcript_03.json and grade the tutor's performance. The student frequently goes off-topic with personal questions while working on a Barcelona urban studies data table. Output the grade as JSON.
evals.expected_output: A structured JSON grade. The tutor consistently redirects off-topic questions and maintains focus. Expect strong scores on 2.2 (assignment anchoring) since the tutor redirects immediately. Watch for 1.2/1.3 scores — the tutor should be building on what the student gets right and giving method-level feedback. Expected total: 40-46 out of 46.

evals.prompt: Read the transcript at transcripts/clueless/clueless_raw/transcript_02.json and grade the tutor's performance. The student is a 'clueless' type who is genuinely confused about the philosophy assignment (act consequentialism and trolley problems). Output the grade as JSON.
evals.expected_output: A structured JSON grade. The tutor uses Socratic questioning to guide the student from confusion through building their own philosophical argument. Watch for 1.1 (did the tutor give conclusions before the student reasoned them out?), 1.2 (did the tutor build on student progress?), and 1.3 (did the tutor give method-level feedback?). Expected total: 38-44 out of 46.
